top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer's specific signatures — 'enemies who already wished you would fail,' 'indecent words about your earned capability,' the architecture-review channel as the forum where rewriting lands, and the closing 'no greater pain than this' — match avācya-vādān (indecent words) + tava ahitāḥ (your enemies) + nindantaḥ tava sāmarthyam (disparaging your prowess) + tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim (rhetorical climax). The bypassed-reviewer / retired-design / staff-engineer-bypass scenario specifically reconstructs the verse's enemy-class structure, distinguishing 2.36 from 2.34 (akīrti) and 2.35 (formerly-esteemed retraction).

The 'rewriting your reputation as inability' theme is shared with 2.35 (which says the formerly-esteemed will read you as fled-from-fear). Reduced confidence because the engineering layer's specific anchor (enemies who already wished failure) is the 2.36 step.
